memory.summary: I barely started the term project due Thursday, so I need to ignore this leisure option and tackle the quick chore first to clear my mind.
That trivial chore barely moved the needle on my term project, yet I wasted time on it. My stress is dropping slightly, but I need to stop stalling and actually start the real work.
I wasted an hour on trivialities while the deadline looms; this procrastination is compounding my stress and will force a terrible crunch later.
Two hours of grinding finally pushed progress to 42%, but my energy is critically low and stress remains high. I need to stop now before the sleep debt makes tomorrow impossible.
Finally moved the needle to 56%, but I'm drained and stressed. This short burst worked, though my energy is dangerously low.
The deadline passed with almost zero progress; wasting time on trivialities while the real task loomed was a costly failure. I need to stop procrastinating immediately and execute.
Two hours of focus just jumped my progress to 93%, proving that immediate execution beats endless planning. With stress down and energy up, I'm ready to close this out before the deadline hits.
That trivial chore was a waste of my prime energy; I should have kept grinding on the main project to secure that 100% finish.
The project is finally 100% done, validating that short-burst execution works better than my usual planning. Now I can drop the stress and move on.

desires: Eliminate the 'quick_chore' immediately to stop the energy bleed on a task with negligible academic value.
Secure a hard boundary against any social requests from cohort_3 to protect my remaining focus capacity for the Term project.

goals.description: Force the 'Trivial easy chore' from 32% to 100% immediately; stop the cognitive dissonance of an incomplete task.
goals.rationale: The Term Project is already done (100%), so this trivial chore is the only remaining variable for a 'win.' My impulsive nature cannot tolerate leaving it at 32%. I need that dopamine hit *now* to clear the board. Relationships and sleep are irrelevant right now; clearing the score is the only metric that matters in this exact second.

goals.description: Aggressively block all social notifications from 'cohort_3' until the chore submission is confirmed.
goals.rationale: Socializing is low value (0.15) compared to the immediate necessity of finishing this task. If they message, I will send a blunt 'Busy, not replying' or ignore them. My assertiveness dictates I set this boundary hard to protect the few minutes left to finish this chore before my focus drifts again.

goals.description: Ignore physical fatigue and sleep needs; push through with high intensity until the chore is marked 'done'.
goals.rationale: Health is negligible (0.13 value). I have no intention of resting yet. Future consequences like burnout are irrelevant compared to the immediate urge to clear this task off the board. I will sacrifice my sleep discipline to ensure the score is updated before I even consider stopping.
